marketState: Neutral, tilted cautiously bullish medium-term — strong weather demand and a crowded COT short offset by a widening storage surplus
summary: Cooling demand is running well above the 5-year normal, especially the 8-16 day window (+21.8%, near the top of its historical range), which is a genuine bullish fundamental into a 3-12 week horizon. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years, a crowded position that adds squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst. That is offset by a bearish seasonality tilt for the next 3-4 weeks (38% probability higher, typical move -1.4%, though reliability is Low this week) and a growing storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, last build a bearish surprise of +22 Bcf). Price is sitting almost exactly at modeled fair value across all horizons (spreads of -$0.01 to -$0.13, mostly FAIR), so there is no near-term valuation edge, and technicals are split between a tentative intraday bullish tilt and a bearish trend structure on the 4h-1w timeframes. Net: medium-term case leans long on weather/positioning, short-term lacks a clean edge.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale out half at TP1, trail stop to breakeven
longTerm.rationale: 8-16 day cooling demand is +21.8% above the 5-year normal and near the top of its historical range (rangePos 0.97), a strong bullish driver over a multi-week horizon, and managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded position vulnerable to a squeeze. This offsets the bearish seasonality tilt for weeks 3-4 (38% probability higher, typical move -1.4%, Low reliability) and the +180 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish weekly surprise.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained cool-down in the 8-16 day forecast back toward/under the 5y normal, and/or continued bearish storage surprises (bigger-than-forecast builds), would remove the bullish drivers and hand control to the seasonality/storage-led bearish case.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price ($2.911) is essentially at modeled fair value (spread -$0.012, status FAIR), so there is no valuation edge for a 1-10 day trade. Technicals are split — 5m/15m show a tentative bullish tilt (EMA 50>200, RSI recovering) while 4h/1d/1w remain in a bearish trend structure (EMA 50<200) — and the futures curve shows mild backwardation into September (-$0.035), a small bearish tell on the prompt month, despite supportive near-term weather (+13.8% demand vs 5y, though only mid-range at rangePos 0.7).
shortTerm.invalidation: Not applicable while waiting; the setup will be reassessed once price breaks decisively out of the current fair-value/technical balance zone.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for a daily close above ~$3.00 with 1h/4h MACD confirming positive (aligns short-term technicals with the bullish weather backdrop) for a long entry, or a daily close below ~$2.75 (confirms the bearish 1d/1w trend and backwardated curve) for a short entry.

keyDrivers: 8-16 day cooling demand +21.8% above 5y normal, near top of its range (rangePos 0.97) — bullish power-burn driver
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range — crowded short, squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst
Storage surplus widening: +180 Bcf vs 5y, last build a bearish surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast)
Price essentially at fair value across all horizons ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair now) — no valuation edge
Seasonality unreliable this week (59pp 10-90% spread) with a bearish 3-4 week tilt (38% prob higher, -1.4% typical move)
risks: 8-16 day weather forecasts can shift quickly; a cool-down would remove the main bullish driver for the long-term trade
Storage surpluses could keep widening if injections keep outpacing forecast, reinforcing the bearish fundamental case
Low seasonality reliability and mixed technicals across timeframes (bullish intraday vs bearish 4h-1w) raise whipsaw risk for any near-term entry
